method: EVIDENCE ONLY. For each plist: parsed with PlistBuddy/plutil; target path existence tested with test -e; load state and last exit code from `launchctl list`; running PID and parentage from `ps -Ao pid,ppid,etime,command`; port bindings from `lsof -nP -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN`; logs tailed where present. NOTHING WAS UNLOADED, DISABLED, KILLED, OR DELETED.
doctrine: No fake green. Where evidence was insufficient the agent is UNKNOWN. No mass-deletion recommendation is made. SAFE_TO_DISABLE is an assessment, not an instruction.

count_correction.actual: 62 com.hoch* plists on disk, 30 loaded. Every classification below is against the real 62.
count_correction.root_cause: The 63/31 figures came from `grep -i hoch`, which also matches `actions.runner.hochster71-hoch_agent_swarm.has-qa-runner-mac.plist` — a GitHub Actions self-hosted runner (PID 893, loaded). It matches only because the REPO NAME contains 'hoch'. It is not a com.hoch.* agent and is out of scope. 62 + 1 = 63 and 30 + 1 = 31, which accounts for the discrepancy exactly.
count_correction.verification: `ls -1 com.hoch*.plist | wc -l` = 62. `ls -1 com.hoch.*.plist | wc -l` = 60 (strict). The 2 remaining are naming outliers: com.hochmesh.autonomous-audit and com.hochster71.has.tracker — both LOADED and both BROKEN, and both would be MISSED by a strict `com.hoch.*` sweep. `launchctl list | awk '{print $3}' | grep '^com\.hoch' | wc -l` = 30.
count_correction.orphan_check: Every one of the 30 loaded labels has a corresponding plist file in ~/Library/LaunchAgents. There are no loaded-but-fileless ghosts.
count_correction.note: ~/Library/LaunchAgents contains 71 plists total; the other 9 are non-hoch and were not in scope.

port_8770_listener.owner_agent: NONE — not launchd-managed; started outside launchd

persistent_scheduler.note: The RUNNING PersistentScheduler is a CHILD of helm_supervisor.py, which restarts it within 5s if it exits.

soak_runner.observed: NO soak_runner.py process is present in the process table.
soak_runner.what_is_running: Two `bash scripts/watch_soak.sh` watcher processes (PID 50254 etime 17:02:01, PID 81170 etime 07:51:36), both with non-launchd parents.
soak_runner.assessment: UNRESOLVED. The brief states a 2-hour soak is running. `ps` shows watchers but no soak_runner.py. This registry does not assert the soak is healthy or unhealthy — it reports only that no soak_runner.py process was observed. Flagged for the caller.
soak_runner.constraint_honored: Nothing was killed or disturbed regardless.

orphan_lease_holders_not_launchd.assessment: Four leftover restart-recovery TEST processes are holding leases via scripts/ag_execution_lease_manager.py and hanging by design ('We will be SIGKILLed -- no cleanup, no release'). They are NOT launchd-managed and are out of scope for this registry, but they touch the lease subsystem the scheduler depends on. Reported, not touched.

interference_risks.risk: RESPAWN — will start a SECOND helm_live_api competing with the running one
interference_risks.evidence: scripts/helm_autoloop.sh is an infinite while-loop (PID 41697, running 17m). Each iteration runs api_up() = `curl -fsS --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:8770/api/v1/helm/wall`. If that health check fails for any reason, start_api() executes `nohup .venv/bin/python3 -m uvicorn backend.helm_live_api:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8770 &`. The currently-running helm_live_api (PID 98242) is bound to 127.0.0.1:8770 and is NOT launchd-managed. If it stalls, hiccups, or returns non-2xx for >5s, helm-autoloop will spawn a competing uvicorn on 0.0.0.0:8770 against the same port.
interference_risks.also: The same loop calls `tailscale serve --bg --https=443 http://127.0.0.1:8770` (external exposure) and `python -m backend.council.notify_founder` (external message delivery) every 60s. Log /tmp/hoch-helm-autoloop.log shows it re-asserting the tailscale :443 route repeatedly (6 entries between 10:26Z and 19:21Z today).
interference_risks.action: DO NOT disable during the soak (it is holding the API up). DO NOT restart the API by hand either — autoloop will race you.

interference_risks.risk: OWNS AND RESPAWNS THE RUNNING SCHEDULER — disabling it kills the soak's scheduler
interference_risks.evidence: backend/mission_control/helm_supervisor.py (PID 12844) spawns and supervises two children: (a) `uvicorn backend.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000` (PID 12846) and (b) `python -c 'from backend.mission_control.persistent_scheduler import PersistentScheduler; PersistentScheduler().run_loop()'` (PID 17807). Its supervisor loop polls every 5s and calls subprocess.Popen again on any child whose poll() is not None. The live PersistentScheduler (17807) is therefore parented to this agent.
interference_risks.action: SAFE_TO_DISABLE = NO. Unloading com.hoch.helm.operations terminates the running persistent scheduler. This is the single most dangerous agent to touch right now.

interference_risks.risk: PORT CONTENTION on :8000 — two live backend.main instances
interference_risks.evidence: lsof confirms TWO listeners on port 8000 simultaneously: PID 11911 (127.0.0.1:8000, child of com.hoch.agent.swarm.runtime via scripts/start_has_runtime.sh -> `uv run uvicorn backend.main:app`) and PID 12846 (*:8000, child of com.hoch.helm.operations). Two independent instances of the same FastAPI app are live. This is the duplicate-telemetry / ambiguous-attribution condition the mission describes, and it is CONFIRMED, not theoretical. launchctl also reports last exit code -9 (SIGKILL) for this agent, with KeepAlive=true — so it has been killed and resurrected at least once.
interference_risks.action: Genuine DUPLICATE, but do not act during the soak. Which of the two instances serves any given client is decided by the kernel's bind precedence (the loopback-specific bind wins for 127.0.0.1 traffic), so telemetry is currently split across two processes.

interference_risks.risk: AUTONOMOUS MUTATION of a protected file — audits backend/mission_control/per_task_lease.py and can queue remediation without asking
interference_risks.evidence: scripts/council_sweep.sh iterates a TARGETS array that explicitly includes `backend/mission_control/per_task_lease.py` (one of the four files this mission forbids modifying) and runs `backend/council/auto_council.py` against each. auto_council.py's docstring states: 'CONFIRMED -> remediation task queued for the autonomous factory' and its code comments read 'remediation is AUTONOMOUS'. It also dispatches to Grok via CouncilDispatchGateway (paid external API + credentials). StartInterval=21600 (every 6h), RunAtLoad=false, currently loaded with last exit 0.
interference_risks.action: FOUNDER_REVIEW. A 6-hourly timer that can queue autonomous edits to the lease manager while a soak depends on it is a real hazard. Not disabled (read-only mission), but the founder should decide.

agents.label: com.hoch.helm.operations
agents.evidence: Running (PID 12844). It is the PARENT of the live PersistentScheduler (PID 17807) and of a backend.main uvicorn (PID 12846, *:8000), and restarts either within 5s of exit. This is the process that makes HELM's scheduler 'always on'.
agents.reason: Unloading it kills the running persistent scheduler that the active soak depends on. Highest-consequence agent on the box.

agents.label: com.hoch.helm-autoloop
agents.evidence: The script's own header records that this file DID NOT EXIST and the plist failed with exit 127 on every boot — the exact dead-target pattern the mission cites. It exists NOW and is running (PID 41697). It is functionally authoritative (keeps helm_live_api :8770 up), but it also performs EXTERNAL SUBMISSION on a 60s loop: `tailscale serve --bg --https=443` (exposes the founder gate on public HTTPS over the tailnet) and `backend.council.notify_founder` (outbound founder notification). External exposure + outbound delivery = FOUNDER_REVIEW by doctrine, and it takes precedence over the AUTHORITATIVE label.
agents.reason: It is currently the only thing that will restart helm_live_api if it dies — but it also owns an internet-reachable :443 route and an outbound notification channel. Disabling it removes API self-healing; leaving it running means it may spawn a competing uvicorn on :8770. See interference_risks rank 1.

agents.label: com.hoch.helm-council
agents.evidence: council_sweep.sh audits a fixed TARGETS list that includes backend/mission_control/per_task_lease.py (a protected file) via backend/council/auto_council.py, which dispatches to Grok (paid external API, credentialed) and whose CONFIRMED findings become 'remediation task queued for the autonomous factory' — i.e. autonomous edits. Fires every 6 hours.
agents.reason: Spends money on external API calls and can autonomously queue edits to the lease manager underneath a live soak. Founder decision, not an ops decision.

agents.label: com.hoch.release.gowatch
agents.evidence: This plist would run the release-signing script in --watch mode with --operator 'Michael Hoch' --confirm HARDCODED, RunAtLoad=true and KeepAlive=true. That is a daemon that continuously signs releases AS THE FOUNDER with the confirmation flag pre-supplied — the human approval step is baked into the plist. It is NOT currently loaded, which is the only reason this is not already an active governance breach.
agents.reason: Do not load this. It pre-authorizes founder release signature in an unattended loop. Founder must explicitly rule on whether this plist should exist at all.

agents.label: com.hoch.ollama.tailscale
agents.evidence: Binds OLLAMA_HOST=100.103.155.4:11434 — a Tailscale IP, not loopback. This exposes a local inference server on the tailnet with no auth layer in the plist. Not currently loaded.
agents.reason: Network exposure decision. Not a HELM dependency (nothing in HELM's running set talks to :11434 right now), but exposing an unauthenticated model endpoint on the tailnet is a founder call.

agents.label: com.hoch.agent.swarm.runtime
agents.evidence: Runs `uv run uvicorn backend.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000` (child PID 11911). com.hoch.helm.operations independently spawns the SAME app on *:8000 (PID 12846). lsof confirms both are LISTENING on port 8000 concurrently. Same job, two owners. Also note plist declares UserName=hoch_agent but the process runs as michaelhoch, and last exit was -9 (SIGKILL) with KeepAlive=true.
agents.reason: It IS a true duplicate of the backend that helm_supervisor already owns — but it is serving live traffic on 127.0.0.1:8000 right now, and 127.0.0.1-specific binds take precedence over the *:8000 bind for loopback clients. Disabling it mid-soak would silently move all loopback traffic to the other instance. Resolve AFTER the soak, and pick ONE owner.

agents.label: com.hoch.has.pert-server
agents.evidence: Running 5d11h (PID 50668), sole listener on 127.0.0.1:8765. The PERT wall depends on this. No competing binder.
agents.reason: Serves the PERT wall. Single owner, healthy, no duplicate.

agents.label: com.hoch.has.live-truth-sidecar
agents.evidence: Running 6d (PID 858). Emits HELM's live truth telemetry — the anti-fake-green surface. Target present, exit 0.
agents.reason: It is the truth channel. Disabling it is exactly how a system starts lying.

agents.label: com.hoch.daemon
agents.evidence: Running 5d17h (PID 78640) in the HELM repo, 10s tick, KeepAlive. Target present, exit 0. Grep confirms it does NOT reference soak_runner, persistent_scheduler, helm_live_api, or per_task_lease — so it is not a competitor to the soak.
agents.reason: Core HELM daemon, healthy, no contention found.

agents.label: com.hoch.goal.runtime.loop
agents.evidence: Running 5d17h (PID 78642). Drives the goal engine on a 60s loop. Target present, exit 0. Grep confirms no reference to the soak/scheduler/lease modules — not a competitor.
agents.reason: Goal-engine liveness depends on it.

agents.label: com.hoch.widget.server
agents.evidence: A bare `python3 -m http.server 8010` serving the frontend/ directory on loopback. Running (PID 896). It is a static file server — no HELM logic, no writes.
agents.reason: Static loopback file server. Nothing in the soak or scheduler path depends on it. Low risk either way, but harmless where it is.

agents.label: com.hoch.health-check
agents.evidence: Machine-level health check in ~/bin, exit 0, 120s interval. Outside the HELM repo; logs to ~/Library/Logs/perf-monitor/. Not HELM-specific.
agents.reason: Host hygiene, not HELM service delivery. Nothing depends on it.

agents.label: com.hoch.memory-monitor
agents.evidence: Host memory-pressure monitor, exit 0, output to /dev/null. Target present.
agents.reason: Host hygiene. But NOTE: it is paired with com.hoch.memory-response, which can take action under memory pressure — see that entry.

agents.label: com.hoch.memory-response
agents.evidence: Runs every 300s, exit 0, target present. Its name implies it TAKES ACTION on memory pressure. Its script lives outside the HELM repo (~/bin/memory-response.sh) and was not read line-by-line in this pass.
agents.reason: PARTIALLY UNVERIFIED: a memory-pressure responder that runs every 5 minutes could, in principle, terminate a memory-heavy process — and the soak/scheduler are long-lived Python processes. I did not confirm that it kills anything, and I will not claim it does. Flagged for a targeted read of ~/bin/memory-response.sh before the next soak.

agents.label: com.hoch.model-upgrade
agents.evidence: THE PLIST IS MALFORMED XML. Line 7 contains a raw, unescaped `&&` inside a <string> element (should be `&&`). Both PlistBuddy ('Error Reading File') and plutil ('Property List error: Encountered unknown ampersand-escape sequence at line 7') REFUSE TO PARSE IT. `launchctl print` shows `program = /bin/bash` with the properties flag `inferred program` — launchd did not get a clean ProgramArguments array. Scheduled for Sunday 03:00; the most recent Sunday was 2026-07-12, but /tmp/hoch-model-upgrade.log has not been written since 2026-07-07. Meanwhile `launchctl list` reports last exit code 0. THAT ZERO IS A FAKE GREEN: an agent that cannot be parsed and has not logged in 7 days is reporting success.
agents.reason: It does not reliably do its job and it is lying about it. The target script scripts/model_upgrade.py DOES exist — so the fix is to repair the plist XML (escape the ampersands), not to delete the script. Residual uncertainty: I cannot prove what launchd actually executes from a plist it half-parsed, which is precisely why this must not stay in production.

agents.label: com.hoch.council-watch
agents.evidence: DEAD TARGET. scripts/council_watch.sh does not exist. launchctl last exit = 127. coordination/council_watch.err.log contains repeated '/bin/bash: .../scripts/council_watch.sh: No such file or directory'. StartInterval=20 means it has been failing every 20 seconds, indefinitely.
agents.reason: Pure dead weight, burning a process spawn every 20s and polluting logs. Nothing to preserve — the script is gone.

agents.label: com.hoch.status-listener
agents.evidence: DEAD TARGET. scripts/status_listener.sh does not exist (test -e fails). Last exit 127 (command not found). Retries every 20s forever.
agents.reason: Dead target, exit 127 on every tick.

agents.label: com.hoch.relay-refresh
agents.evidence: DEAD TARGET. scripts/relay_refresh.sh does not exist. Last exit 127. Retries every 60s.
agents.reason: Dead target, exit 127 on every tick.

agents.label: com.hoch.rollcall-digest
agents.evidence: DEAD TARGET. scripts/status_notify.sh does not exist. Last exit 127. Scheduled 3x/day (07:00, 13:00, 19:00) — so the founder's thrice-daily rollcall digest HAS NEVER BEEN DELIVERED from this agent. A notification channel that silently does not exist is worse than none.
agents.reason: Dead target. But FLAG: if the founder believes he is receiving a 3x-daily rollcall digest, he is not. Confirm whether that digest is delivered by some other path before retiring this.

agents.label: com.hoch.frontier-council
agents.evidence: DEAD TARGET. scripts/frontier_council_caller.py does not exist. Last exit 2 (python: can't open file). Fires every 30 min.
agents.reason: Dead target. Note: had it worked, a 'frontier council caller' would be dispatching to paid external models every 30 minutes — so this being broken has been silently SAVING money, and re-enabling it would be a FOUNDER_REVIEW event, not a fix.

agents.label: com.hoch.mesh-broker
agents.evidence: DEAD TARGET. /Users/michaelhoch/mesh-os/mesh_broker.py does not exist. Last exit 2. KeepAlive=true means launchd is respawn-looping a missing file. ALSO: the plist hardcodes MESH_API_KEY=mesh-local-key in plaintext.
agents.reason: Dead target in a KeepAlive respawn loop. Secondary finding: a credential (however weak) is stored in cleartext in a launchd plist — worth the founder's attention as a pattern.

agents.label: com.hochmesh.autonomous-audit
agents.evidence: DEAD TARGET. mesh-os/scripts/mesh_autonomous_cycle.sh does not exist. Last exit 127. Fires every 15 min. Note the label is com.hochmesh.* not com.hoch.* — it is easy to miss in a com.hoch.* sweep.
agents.reason: Dead target, and its entire parent tree (mesh-os) is missing its scripts.

agents.label: com.hochster71.has.tracker
agents.evidence: DEAD TARGET. WorkingDirectory is /Users/michaelhoch/hoch_agent_swarm/has_live_project_tracker and it runs `node server.js`, but that server.js does not exist. Last exit 1, KeepAlive=true — an infinite respawn of a missing entrypoint. Label is com.hochster71.* — another naming outlier.
agents.reason: Dead target inside the HELM repo, respawning forever. This one is INSIDE hoch_agent_swarm, so it is the most likely of the broken set to be mistaken for a live HELM service.

agents.label: com.hoch.family.morning
agents.evidence: Target script EXISTS, but last exit code is 141 = 128+13 = terminated by SIGPIPE. It is not completing successfully. Runs daily at 07:30 from the legacy hoch-swarm repo (NOT the HELM repo).
agents.reason: Exits nonzero (SIGPIPE), so the morning family brief is not reliably produced. It belongs to the separate 'family ops' product line, not HELM — the founder should say whether that line is still wanted before anything is retired.

agents.label: com.hoch.family.evening
agents.evidence: Runs the EXACT SAME script with the EXACT SAME arguments as com.hoch.family.morning — `cd /Users/michaelhoch/hoch-swarm && ./scripts/family_daily_brief_cycle.sh`. The only difference is the trigger time (20:30 vs 07:30). Both also fail identically with exit 141 (SIGPIPE). Two labels, one job, zero successes.
agents.reason: It is a literal duplicate of family.morning (same command line) AND it is failing. Whether the family-ops line should run twice daily at all is a founder call.

agents.label: com.hoch.family.neo.ops.seeder
agents.evidence: Target exists but last exit code is 1 (failure). Fires every 15 min and fails every 15 min. It ENQUEUES work ('sentinel_enqueue') into the legacy hoch-swarm queue.
agents.reason: Failing on every run. It is a work-ENQUEUEING agent, so if it ever started succeeding it would begin injecting tasks into the legacy family-ops queue — a behavior change the founder should sanction, not something to silently 'fix'.

agents.label: com.hoch.family.productionbrief
agents.evidence: Target exists, last exit 0 (the only family.* agent that is actually succeeding). Daily 07:45, legacy hoch-swarm repo.
agents.reason: It works. It belongs to the family-ops product line, not HELM. Retiring a working daily brief is a product decision, not an ops cleanup.

agents.label: com.hoch.agent.event.server
agents.evidence: Running (PID 878), exit 0, target exists. Part of the legacy hoch-swarm/family_ops stack, NOT the HELM repo. It is genuinely alive and healthy.
agents.reason: Healthy and running, but serves the family-ops line rather than HELM. It is a live event server — other family_ops agents likely depend on it. Do not retire without mapping its consumers.

agents.label: com.hoch.agent.tool.bridge
agents.evidence: Target exists, exit 0, fires every 120s in the legacy family_ops tree. It is an agent TOOL EXECUTION bridge ('tool-cycle') — i.e. it runs agent tool calls on a timer.
agents.reason: Working, but it EXECUTES AGENT TOOL CYCLES every 2 minutes in a repo nobody is watching. Not a soak competitor (different repo, different queue), but an unattended executor deserves the founder's eye.

agents.label: com.hoch.mesh.agent-scheduler
agents.evidence: Running (PID 899), 30s tick, target exists. NAME COLLISION WARNING: this is an 'agent-scheduler', but it is NOT HELM's PersistentScheduler. It belongs to hasf-mesh-studio, talks to the mesh API on :8789, and shares no code, queue, or lease store with backend/mission_control/persistent_scheduler.py. It is not a soak competitor.
agents.reason: Healthy, but from the mesh product line. Called out explicitly because its NAME will make an incident responder think it is HELM's scheduler. It is not.

agents.label: com.hoch.mesh.learning
agents.evidence: Running (PID 902), exit 0, target exists. Mesh learning daemon (retrains at HOCH_MESH_TRAIN_THRESHOLD=25, 300s interval). Separate mesh product line.
agents.reason: Healthy mesh-line daemon. No HELM dependency observed.

agents.label: com.hoch.mesh.service
agents.evidence: Currently running (PID 11335) but launchctl reports last exit code 1 with KeepAlive=true — meaning it has crashed at least once and been resurrected. Target exists. Mesh 24x7 service.
agents.reason: Alive but with a crash in its history (last exit 1). Mesh product line, not HELM. Worth a look at .hoch-mesh/academy/mesh-24x7/logs/service.err.log to see why it died.

agents.label: com.hoch.api.server
agents.evidence: A THIRD plist for the same backend.main:app on port 8000 — identical in purpose to com.hoch.agent.swarm.runtime (which serves it on 127.0.0.1:8000) and to the uvicorn child spawned by com.hoch.helm.operations (which serves it on *:8000). It is NOT loaded, which is fortunate: loading it would create a third competitor for port 8000.
agents.reason: Already unloaded and redundant three ways over. Do NOT load it. It is the cleanest illustration of the ambiguity this registry exists to kill: three plists, one port, no declared owner.

agents.label: com.hoch.brain.cadence
agents.evidence: Target EXISTS in the HELM repo and the plist is well-formed, but the agent is NOT LOADED — so it has never been contributing. Grep confirms brain_cadence.sh does not reference soak_runner, persistent_scheduler, helm_live_api, or per_task_lease.
agents.reason: Not loaded, so disabling changes nothing. Listed as SUPPORTING rather than LEGACY because the script is present and current in the HELM repo — someone may have intended this to run. Confirm intent before deleting the plist.

agents.label: com.hoch.live-swarm
agents.evidence: Points at /Users/michaelhoch/hoch-agent-swarm/ — a THIRD swarm repo, distinct from both hoch_agent_swarm (HELM) and hoch-swarm (family ops). Target exists but the agent is not loaded and nothing on :8080 is listening.
agents.reason: Not loaded, and from a third abandoned repo lineage. The existence of three sibling repos (hoch_agent_swarm, hoch-swarm, hoch-agent-swarm) with near-identical names is itself an incident-attribution hazard worth recording.

agents.label: com.hoch.family.ui
agents.evidence: Target exists; not loaded; nothing listening on :8797. Family-ops dashboard from the superseded hoch-swarm repo.
agents.reason: Not loaded. Family-ops line.

agents.label: com.hoch.family.supervisor
agents.evidence: Target exists; NOT loaded. A supervisor (30s interval per HAM_FAMILY_SUPERVISOR_INTERVAL) for the family-ops stack.
agents.reason: Not loaded. NOTE: if it WERE loaded it would be a second supervisor on the box alongside helm_supervisor — keep it unloaded.

agents.label: com.hoch.family.watchdog
agents.evidence: Target exists; not loaded. Family-ops watchdog, 300s.
agents.reason: Not loaded. Family-ops line. A watchdog that is not running is not protecting anything — do not assume coverage from its presence on disk.

agents.evidence: Target exists; not loaded. Would bind 0.0.0.0:8810 (all interfaces) to serve work-pull to a Dell node.
agents.reason: Not loaded. Family-ops distributed-node line. Binds 0.0.0.0 — if revived, that is a network-exposure decision.

agents.label: com.hoch.phase73b.factory.tick
agents.evidence: Target exists; NOT loaded. A 'factory tick' — the family_ops analogue of HELM's factory loop.
agents.reason: Not loaded. FLAG: this is a FACTORY TICK. If loaded, it would drive a second, ungoverned agent factory every 60s alongside HELM's. Keep it unloaded.

agents.label: com.hoch.phase73e.mesh.live.audit
agents.evidence: Target exists; not loaded.
agents.reason: Not loaded. Family-ops phase artifact.

agents.label: com.hoch.mesh.api
agents.evidence: Target exists; NOT loaded — yet com.hoch.mesh.agent-scheduler IS loaded and running with API_PORT=8789 pointing at this very API. The scheduler is talking to an API whose launchd agent is not loaded.
agents.reason: Not loaded. BUT FLAG A DEPENDENCY INVERSION: mesh.agent-scheduler (loaded, PID 899) expects the mesh API on :8789, and this — the plist that would serve it — is unloaded. Either the API is being started some other way, or the running scheduler is failing to reach it. Worth one check on the mesh line.

agents.reason: Not loaded. NOTE: com.hoch.mesh.service IS loaded and has crashed at least once (last exit 1) — and the watchdog that would have caught that is unloaded.

agents.reason: Not loaded, and duplicative of the other mesh dashboard agents. Consolidate the mesh dashboard story before reviving any of them.

unresolved.item: soak_runner.py not observed in the process table
unresolved.detail: The mission brief states a 2-hour soak is running and must not be disturbed. Nothing was disturbed. However, `ps -Ao pid,ppid,etime,command | grep -i soak` returned ONLY two `bash scripts/watch_soak.sh` watcher processes (PIDs 50254 and 81170) and no soak_runner.py process. I am reporting this rather than asserting the soak is healthy. Verify before relying on the soak's output.

The plist is malformed XML and neither plutil nor PlistBuddy will parse it. launchd reports `inferred program = /bin/bash` and last exit 0. I cannot determine with certainty what launchd actually executes from a plist it only half-parsed. Classified BROKEN on the strength of the parse failure plus a 7-day-stale log against a weekly schedule, but the precise runtime behavior is genuinely UNKNOWN.

com.hoch.memory-response runs every 300s and its name implies it takes action under memory pressure. Its script lives outside the HELM repo and was not read line-by-line. If it terminates memory-heavy processes, it is a latent threat to long-running soak/scheduler processes. NOT CONFIRMED — flagged for a targeted read, not asserted.

The live API on 127.0.0.1:8770 is not managed by any of the 63 plists. It was started outside launchd. com.hoch.helm-autoloop will try to (re)start it on 0.0.0.0:8770 if its health check fails. Ownership of the :8770 service is therefore ambiguous by construction.

unresolved.item: Whether the hoch-swarm (family_ops) product line is still wanted
unresolved.detail: 26 of the 63 plists belong to /Users/michaelhoch/hoch-swarm or the mesh trees, not to HELM. Their targets mostly exist. Whether they are LEGACY-to-retire or an active second product is a founder question, not something evidence on disk can settle. They are marked LEGACY where unloaded and SUPPORTING where healthy and running, with safe_to_disable=needs-founder on every running one.

recommendation.no_mass_deletion: This registry does NOT recommend bulk removal. Nothing here was disabled or deleted.
recommendation.zero_risk_now: The 10 BROKEN agents with confirmed dead targets are burning process spawns and writing exit-127 noise on timers as short as 20s. They can be retired with no service impact — but only AFTER the soak completes, and rollcall-digest should first be checked against whatever the founder believes is delivering his 3x-daily digest.
founder_decisions_pending: com.hoch.release.gowatch (pre-authorized founder signature in an unattended loop — do not load)
com.hoch.helm-council (spends money, can autonomously edit per_task_lease.py)
com.hoch.ollama.tailscale (unauthenticated model endpoint on the tailnet)
recommendation.structural: Port 8000 has TWO live owners and a THIRD unloaded plist claiming it. Pick one owner for backend.main and delete the ambiguity — that single change removes the duplicate-telemetry and incident-attribution problem this mission was called to solve.
